title: Ensure that the scheduler.conf file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the scheduler.conf file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the scheduler.conf file ownership is set to root:root.
input_row.rationale: The scheduler.conf file is the kubeconfig file for the Scheduler. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf Verify that the ownership is set to root:root.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf Default Value: By default, scheduler.conf file ownership is set to root:root. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubeadm/

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the Control Plane node where the Kubernetes Scheduler is running.
2. Run the command: `stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf`
3. Verify that the output shows `root:root`.
4. If the ownership is not set to `root:root`, run the command: `chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/scheduler.conf` to remediate the issue.
